### Post by PokeParadox on 2009-03-21
I'm trying to get games to display on my HDTV only.
Currently they are appearing centred between my laptop screen and TV.
I'm probably doing it all wrong, I've read so many posts about this it's unreal...

Any help, as always, greatly appreciated!

Here is my current xorg.conf
```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Mon Nov  3 08:46:04 UTC 2008
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder58)  Tue Nov  4 17:18:57 PST 2008

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier     "Monitor0"
	VendorName     "Unknown"
	ModelName      "DFP-0"
	HorizSync       28.0 - 55.0
	VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
	ModeLine       "1920x1080@60i" 77.60 1920 1952 2240 2272 1080 1104 1110 1135 interlace +vsync +hsync
	ModeLine       "1280x720_TV" 74.2500 1205 1355 1395 1650 688 708 714 750 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine       "1280x720@60" 73.78 1280 1312 1592 1624 720 735 742 757 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine       "1024x768@60" 64.56 1024 1056 1296 1328 768 783 791 807 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine       "800x600@60" 38.21 800 832 976 1008 600 612 618 631 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine       "640x480@60" 24.11 640 672 760 792 480 490 495 505 +hsync +vsync
	Option         "DPMS"
	Option         "ExactModeTimingsDVI" "TRUE"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier     "Screen0"
	Device         "Device0"
	Monitor        "Monitor0"
	Option         "TwinView" "1"
	Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-1"
	Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: nvidia-auto-select +0+688, DFP-1: 1280x720_TV +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: 1280x720_TV +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: 1280x720@60 +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: 1024x768@60 +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: 800x600@60 +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: 640x480@60 +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: 1290x1080@60i +0+0;
DFP-0: NULL, DFP-1: nvidia-auto-select +0+0;
DFP-0: nvidia-auto-select, DFP-1:  NULL+0+0"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport 0 0
		Modes "1920x1080@60i" "1280x720_TV" "1280x720@60" "1024x768@60" "800x600@60" "640x480@60"
		Depth       24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load           "dbe"
	Load           "extmod"
	Load           "type1"
	Load           "freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Disable	"dri2"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier     "Mouse0"
	Driver         "mouse"
	Option         "Protocol" "auto"
	Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
	Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver         "synaptics"
	Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option         "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
	Option         "SHMConfig" "on"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier     "Keyboard0"
	Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Layout0"
	Screen         "Screen0"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier     "Device0"
	VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
	BoardName      "GeForce 8600M GS"
	Option         "UseEDID" "TRUE"
	Option "NoLogo" "1"
	Option "Coolbits" "1"
	Driver	"nvidia"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

```

### Post by wolfen69 on 2009-03-21
did you do
```
sudo nvidia-settings
```
and configure the displays there?

---

### Post by PokeParadox on 2009-03-22
That's how I started off.

Then I added the custom ModeLine 1280x720_TV that fixed my overscan issues.
Now the problem is I can't start a game only on the top screen(my HDTV).

I don't want to undo what is already working, but I want common game resolutions to be displayed only on the top screen and not spanning both.

---
